5||[[清水山古墳]]||前方後円墳||57m|| ||rowspan=2|6c中葉-後半||（消滅） |- |段ノ山古墳||前方後円墳|| || ||（消滅） |- |'''双ヶ岡1号墳'''||[[円墳]]||45m||15.8m{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}||rowspan=2|6世紀後半||なし |- |衣笠山1号墳||円墳||26m||(6.0m)||なし |- |rowspan=6|6||[[蛇塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||75m||17.8m||rowspan=6|6c末-7c初頭||国の史跡 |- |[[円山古墳 (京都市)|円山古墳
（大覚寺1号墳）]]||円墳||50m||14.7m||[[陵墓参考地]] |- |[[狐塚古墳 (京都市)|狐塚古墳
（大覚寺4号墳）]]||円墳||28m||(12.8m)||なし |- |[[入道塚古墳|入道塚古墳
（大覚寺2号墳）]]||[[方墳]]||30m|||11.2m||陵墓参考地 |- |[[大覚寺古墳群|南天塚古墳
（大覚寺3号墳）]]||円墳||25-30m||8.1m||（埋没） |- |御堂ヶ池1号墳||円墳||30m||(8.3m)||京都市登録史跡 |- |rowspan=2|7||[[甲塚古墳 (京都市)|甲塚古墳]]||円墳||38m||14.4m||rowspan=2|7c前半||なし |- |広沢古墳||円墳||30m||12.0m||なし |}
[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、[[双ヶ丘]]の一ノ丘頂上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。双ヶ丘では、一ノ丘と二ノ丘の間の谷筋に9基、二ノ丘の頂上に1基、二ノ丘南裾から三ノ丘に13基の古墳計24基が分布するが、本古墳はそのうちで最大規模になる{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 墳形は円形で、直径44メートル・高さ8メートルを測る{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室全長15.8メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品の大部分は失われており、石室内の調査では石棺片のほか、金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]に先行する時期の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。なお、戦前に[[清原夏野]]（[[837年]]）の墓として顕彰されたことで、古墳前に石碑が建てられている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 === 来歴 === * [[1912年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[清原夏野]]墓碑の建立{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）11月13日、国の[[名勝]]「雙ヶ岡」の指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1698|雙ヶ岡}}。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）、京都市による双ヶ丘の公有化（以後環境整備）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、双ヶ岡1号墳の環境整備に伴う発掘調査（京都市文化観光局、[[1981年]]に報告）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、双ヶ丘の一般開放{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|旧石室開口部（現在は閉塞）}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * 石室全長：15.8メートル * 玄室：長さ6.4メートル、幅4.0メートル、高さ5.2メートル（報告書では長さ6.1メートル、幅3.6メートル、高さ5.0メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ2.3メートル（報告書では長さ8.5メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ2.3メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） 玄室の奥壁は各1石の3段積み、側壁は4段積み、前壁は2段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1段、羨道の側壁は推定2段積みである{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。玄室右壁の一部が崩れて天井石の一部が傾くなど崩壊する危険があるため、調査後に玄室部分には土嚢が充填されている。 石室内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では[[凝灰岩]]製石棺片のほか金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のほか、[[平安時代]]の土器が検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京都市設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/news/leaflet/268.pdf 「双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓（リーフレット京都No.268　考古アラカルト51）」]}}京都市埋蔵文化財研究所・京都市考古資料館、2011年|ref={{Harvid|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}}}。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=双ヶ丘古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|双ヶ丘古墳群（平凡社）|1979}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=[[広瀬和雄]]|year=2013|chapter=山城・蛇塚古墳をめぐる二,三の問題|title=国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第178集|publisher=[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|広瀬和雄|2013}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[京都大学]]考古学研究会|author=|year=1971|chapter=各古墳の概要 > 双ケ岡1号墳|title=嵯峨野の古墳時代 -御堂ヶ池群集墳発掘調査報告-|publisher=京大考古学研究会出版事務局|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=京都市|author=|year=1983|chapter=古墳時代 > 右京区|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[双ヶ丘]] == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Narabigaoka Kofun Group|双ヶ岡古墳群}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ [[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、[[双ヶ丘]]の一ノ丘頂上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。双ヶ丘では、一ノ丘と二ノ丘の間の谷筋に9基、二ノ丘の頂上に1基、二ノ丘南裾から三ノ丘に13基の古墳計24基が分布するが、本古墳はそのうちで最大規模になる{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 墳形は円形で、直径44メートル・高さ8メートルを測る{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室全長15.8メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品の大部分は失われており、石室内の調査では石棺片のほか、金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]に先行する時期の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。なお、戦前に[[清原夏野]]（[[837年]]）の墓として顕彰されたことで、古墳前に石碑が建てられている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 === 来歴 === * [[1912年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[清原夏野]]墓碑の建立{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）11月13日、国の[[名勝]]「雙ヶ岡」の指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1698|雙ヶ岡}}。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）、京都市による双ヶ丘の公有化（以後環境整備）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、双ヶ岡1号墳の環境整備に伴う発掘調査（京都市文化観光局、[[1981年]]に報告）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、双ヶ丘の一般開放{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|旧石室開口部（現在は閉塞）}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * 石室全長：15.8メートル * 玄室：長さ6.4メートル、幅4.0メートル、高さ5.2メートル（報告書では長さ6.1メートル、幅3.6メートル、高さ5.0メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ2.3メートル（報告書では長さ8.5メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ2.3メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） 玄室の奥壁は各1石の3段積み、側壁は4段積み、前壁は2段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1段、羨道の側壁は推定2段積みである{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。玄室右壁の一部が崩れて天井石の一部が傾くなど崩壊する危険があるため、調査後に玄室部分には土嚢が充填されている。 石室内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では[[凝灰岩]]製石棺片のほか金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のほか、[[平安時代]]の土器が検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京都市設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** [[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、[[双ヶ丘]]の一ノ丘頂上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。双ヶ丘では、一ノ丘と二ノ丘の間の谷筋に9基、二ノ丘の頂上に1基、二ノ丘南裾から三ノ丘に13基の古墳計24基が分布するが、本古墳はそのうちで最大規模になる{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 墳形は円形で、直径44メートル・高さ8メートルを測る{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室全長15.8メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品の大部分は失われており、石室内の調査では石棺片のほか、金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]に先行する時期の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。なお、戦前に[[清原夏野]]（[[837年]]）の墓として顕彰されたことで、古墳前に石碑が建てられている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 === 来歴 === * [[1912年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[清原夏野]]墓碑の建立{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）11月13日、国の[[名勝]]「雙ヶ岡」の指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1698|雙ヶ岡}}。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）、京都市による双ヶ丘の公有化（以後環境整備）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、双ヶ岡1号墳の環境整備に伴う発掘調査（京都市文化観光局、[[1981年]]に報告）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、双ヶ丘の一般開放{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|旧石室開口部（現在は閉塞）}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * 石室全長：15.8メートル * 玄室：長さ6.4メートル、幅4.0メートル、高さ5.2メートル（報告書では長さ6.1メートル、幅3.6メートル、高さ5.0メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ2.3メートル（報告書では長さ8.5メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ2.3メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） 玄室の奥壁は各1石の3段積み、側壁は4段積み、前壁は2段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1段、羨道の側壁は推定2段積みである{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。玄室右壁の一部が崩れて天井石の一部が傾くなど崩壊する危険があるため、調査後に玄室部分には土嚢が充填されている。 石室内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では[[凝灰岩]]製石棺片のほか金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のほか、[[平安時代]]の土器が検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京都市設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** [[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、[[双ヶ丘]]の一ノ丘頂上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。双ヶ丘では、一ノ丘と二ノ丘の間の谷筋に9基、二ノ丘の頂上に1基、二ノ丘南裾から三ノ丘に13基の古墳計24基が分布するが、本古墳はそのうちで最大規模になる{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 墳形は円形で、直径44メートル・高さ8メートルを測る{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室全長15.8メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品の大部分は失われており、石室内の調査では石棺片のほか、金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]に先行する時期の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。なお、戦前に[[清原夏野]]（[[837年]]）の墓として顕彰されたことで、古墳前に石碑が建てられている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 === 来歴 === * [[1912年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[清原夏野]]墓碑の建立{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）11月13日、国の[[名勝]]「雙ヶ岡」の指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1698|雙ヶ岡}}。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）、京都市による双ヶ丘の公有化（以後環境整備）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、双ヶ岡1号墳の環境整備に伴う発掘調査（京都市文化観光局、[[1981年]]に報告）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、双ヶ丘の一般開放{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|旧石室開口部（現在は閉塞）}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * 石室全長：15.8メートル * 玄室：長さ6.4メートル、幅4.0メートル、高さ5.2メートル（報告書では長さ6.1メートル、幅3.6メートル、高さ5.0メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ2.3メートル（報告書では長さ8.5メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ2.3メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） 玄室の奥壁は各1石の3段積み、側壁は4段積み、前壁は2段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1段、羨道の側壁は推定2段積みである{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。玄室右壁の一部が崩れて天井石の一部が傾くなど崩壊する危険があるため、調査後に玄室部分には土嚢が充填されている。 石室内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では[[凝灰岩]]製石棺片のほか金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のほか、[[平安時代]]の土器のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京都市設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** [[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、[[双ヶ丘]]の一ノ丘頂上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。双ヶ丘では、一ノ丘と二ノ丘の間の谷筋に9基、二ノ丘の頂上に1基、二ノ丘南裾から三ノ丘に13基の古墳計24基が分布するが、本古墳はそのうちで最大規模になる{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 墳形は円形で、直径44メートル・高さ8メートルを測る{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室全長15.8メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品の大部分は失われており、石室内の調査では石棺片のほか、金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]に先行する時期の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。なお、戦前に[[清原夏野]]（[[837年]]）の墓として顕彰されたことで、古墳前に石碑が建てられている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 === 来歴 === * [[1912年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[清原夏野]]墓碑の建立{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）11月13日、国の[[名勝]]「雙ヶ岡」の指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1698|雙ヶ岡}}。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）、京都市による双ヶ丘の公有化（以後環境整備）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、双ヶ岡1号墳の環境整備に伴う発掘調査（京都市文化観光局、[[1981年]]に報告）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、双ヶ丘の一般開放{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|旧石室開口部（現在は閉塞）}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * 石室全長：15.8メートル * 玄室：長さ6.4メートル、幅4.0メートル、高さ5.2メートル（報告書では長さ6.1メートル、幅3.6メートル、高さ5.0メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ2.3メートル（報告書では長さ8.5メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ2.3メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） 玄室の奥壁は各1石の3段積み、側壁は4段積み、前壁は2段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1段、羨道の側壁は推定2段積みである{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。玄室右壁の一部が崩れて天井石の一部が傾くなど崩壊する危険があるため、調査後に玄室部分には土嚢が充填されている。 石室内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では[[凝灰岩]]製石棺片のほか金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のほか、[[平安時代]]の土器のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京都市設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=双ヶ丘古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|双ヶ丘古墳群（平凡社）|1979}}}} ** {{Cite 右京区|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[双ヶ丘]] == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Narabigaoka Kofun Group|双ヶ岡古墳群}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ 1号墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|0|50|N|135|41|50|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|0|59.81|N|135|41|59.30|E}} | label1=垂箕山 | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-東南東.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|0|25.57|N|135|42|43.45|E}} | label2=天塚 | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|0|30.51|N|135|42|44.42|E}} | label3=清水山 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|0|14.80|N|135|42|24.37|E}} | label4=段ノ山 | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|1|26.80|N|135|42|47.87|E}} | label5=双ヶ岡1号 | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|0|42.83|N|135|41|59.88|E}} | label6=蛇塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size6=20 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | label7=円山 | label-pos7=left | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=20 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|35|1|10.80|N|135|41|24.85|E}} | label8=甲塚 | label-pos8=left | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=20 }} '''双ヶ岡1号墳'''（ならびがおかいちごうふん、双ヶ丘1号墳）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]御室双岡町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。双ヶ岡古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==

・
5||[[清水山古墳]]||前方後円墳||57m|| ||rowspan=2|6c中葉-後半||（消滅） |- |段ノ山古墳||前方後円墳|| || ||（消滅） |- |'''双ヶ岡1号墳'''||[[円墳]]||44m||15.8m{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}||rowspan=2|6世紀後半||なし |- |衣笠山1号墳||円墳||26m||(6.0m)||なし |- |rowspan=6|6||[[蛇塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||75m||17.8m||rowspan=6|6c末-7c初頭||国の史跡 |- |[[円山古墳 (京都市)|円山古墳

（大覚寺2号墳）]]||[[方墳]]||30m|||11.2m||陵墓参考地 |- |[[大覚寺古墳群|南天塚古墳
（大覚寺3号墳）]]||円墳||25-30m||8.1m||（埋没） |- |御堂ヶ池1号墳||円墳||30m||(8.3m)||京都市登録史跡 |- |rowspan=2|7||[[甲塚古墳 (京都市)|甲塚古墳]]||円墳||38m||14.4m||rowspan=2|7c前半||なし |- |広沢古墳||円墳||30m||12.0m||なし |}
[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、[[双ヶ丘]]の一ノ丘頂上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。双ヶ丘では、一ノ丘と二ノ丘の間の谷筋に9基、二ノ丘の頂上に1基、二ノ丘南裾から三ノ丘に13基の古墳計24基が分布するが、本古墳はそのうちで最大規模になる{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 墳形は円形で、直径44メートル・高さ8メートルを測る{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室全長15.8メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品の大部分は失われており、石室内の調査では石棺片のほか、金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]に先行する時期の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。なお、戦前に[[清原夏野]]（[[837年]]）の墓として顕彰されたことで、古墳前に石碑が建てられている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1912年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[清原夏野]]墓碑の建立{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）11月13日、国の[[名勝]]「雙ヶ岡」の指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1698|雙ヶ岡}}。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）、京都市による双ヶ丘の公有化（以後環境整備）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、双ヶ岡1号墳の環境整備に伴う発掘調査（京都市文化観光局、[[1981年]]に報告）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、双ヶ丘の一般開放{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|旧石室開口部（現在は閉塞）}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * 石室全長：15.8メートル * 玄室：長さ6.4メートル、幅4.0メートル、高さ5.2メートル（報告書では長さ6.1メートル、幅3.6メートル、高さ5.0メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ2.3メートル（報告書では長さ8.5メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ2.3メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） 玄室の奥壁は各1石の3段積み、側壁は4段積み、前壁は2段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1段、羨道の側壁は推定2段積みである{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。玄室右壁の一部が崩れて天井石の一部が傾くなど崩壊する危険があるため、調査後に玄室部分には土嚢が充填されている。 石室内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では[[凝灰岩]]製石棺片のほか金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のほか、[[平安時代]]の土器のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京都市設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=双ヶ丘古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|双ヶ丘古墳群（平凡社）|1979}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=[[広瀬和雄]]|year=2013|chapter=山城・蛇塚古墳をめぐる二,三の問題|title=国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第178集|publisher=[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|広瀬和雄|2013}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite 右京区|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[双ヶ丘]] == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Narabigaoka Kofun Group|双ヶ岡古墳群}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ Part of the Narabigaoka Kofun Group. == Overview == [[Kyoto Basin]] (Yamashiro Basin), west of [[Sakyo Ward]], houses the Narabigaoka 1-gō Kofun at the summit of Narabigaoka's Ichino-oka (First Hill). This large round burial mound is the most prominent in the Narabigaoka Kofun Group, which comprises 24 mou..."
revision.text:
'''Narabigaoka 1-gō Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳)
* {{ill|Narabigaoka 1-gō Kofun|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳}} - A kofun located in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. Part of the Narabigaoka Kofun Group.
== Overview == [[Kyoto Basin]] (Yamashiro Basin), west of [[Sakyo Ward]], houses the Narabigaoka 1-gō Kofun at the summit of Narabigaoka's Ichino-oka (First Hill). This large round burial mound is the most prominent in the Narabigaoka Kofun Group, which comprises 24 mounds distributed across the area, including Ichino-oka and Nino-oka (Second Hill). Excavations conducted in 1980 revealed the burial's layout, and findings highlighted connections to the [[Hata clan]], a prominent immigrant family in the region.
### Key Dimensions - Shape: Round burial mound (円墳) - Diameter: 44 meters - Height: 8 meters - Burial Facility: Double-sleeved stone chamber (横穴式石室) constructed with large stones. - Total Chamber Length: 15.8 meters
== Excavations and Findings == The chamber, once targeted by looters, contained limited relics, including fragments of a tuff coffin, gold rings, iron artifacts, and pottery shards (Sue ware and Haji ware). Based on construction style and artifacts, the kofun dates back to the late [[6th century]] during the Kofun period.
== Historical Context == The mound, speculated to belong to a local ruler connected with the Hata clan, is an early example of large burial mounds preceding more complex tombs like the [[Hebizuka Kofun]].
### Cultural and Preservation Milestones - **1912 (Meiji 12):** Monument erected to honor the tomb as the burial site of [[Kiyohara no Natsuno]]. - **1941:** Narabigaoka designated a National Scenic Site (名勝) by the Japanese government. - **1980:** Excavation and environmental preservation undertaken by Kyoto City's cultural authorities.
== References == {{small|Sources used for this article.}} * Local Government Publications: - {{Cite book|author=Kyoto City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Narabigaoka 1-gō Kofun Excavation and Preservation Project Report|year=1981}} * Academic Research: - {{Cite book|author=Hirose Kazuo|title=Issues Surrounding the Hebizuka Kofun in Yamashiro|year=2013}}
== Related Articles == * [[Narabigaoka Kofun Group]]
== External Links == * {{Commonscat|Narabigaoka Kofun Group|双ヶ岡古墳群}}

'''Narabigaoka 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳)
* {{ill|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳}} - A kofun located in Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. Part of the Narabigaoka Kofun Group.
== Overview == The Narabigaoka 1 Kofun is a large circular kofun constructed on the peak of the first hill of Narabigaoka in Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City. It is the largest kofun within the Narabigaoka Kofun Group, which consists of 24 burial mounds distributed across the hills of Narabigaoka.
The burial mound has a diameter of 44 meters and a height of 8 meters. The burial chamber is a lateral stone corridor with a total length of 15.8 meters. The chamber uses massive stones for construction and faces southwest. Artifacts such as fragments of stone coffins, gold rings, iron items, Sue ware, and Haji ware were discovered during excavations.
The construction of this kofun is estimated to date back to the late 6th century, during the latter part of the Kofun period. It is believed to have served as the burial site of a prominent local clan, possibly associated with the immigrant Hata clan, known for their role in developing the Uzumasa and Saga regions.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a lateral stone corridor, and its specifications are as follows: * Total length: 15.8 meters * Main chamber: 6.4 meters long, 4.0 meters wide, and 5.2 meters high * Corridor: 9.2 meters long, 2.3 meters wide, and 2.3 meters high
== Excavation History == * 1912: A tombstone was erected in honor of Kiyohara no Natsuno. * 1980: Kyoto City conducted excavation and preservation work on the kofun.
== References == * Kyoto City Preservation Reports (1981). * National Museum of Japanese History research on the kofun.
== Related Links == * {{Commonscat|Narabigaoka Kofun Group|双ヶ岡古墳群}}
[[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Burial Mounds in Japan]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]]

'''Narabigaoka 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳)
* {{ill|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳}} - A kofun located in Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. Part of the Narabigaoka Kofun Group.
== Overview == The Narabigaoka 1 Kofun is a large circular kofun constructed on the peak of the first hill of Narabigaoka in Kyoto Prefecture, Kyoto City. It is the largest kofun within the Narabigaoka Kofun Group, which consists of 24 burial mounds distributed across the hills of Narabigaoka.
The burial mound has a diameter of 44 meters and a height of 8 meters. The burial chamber is a lateral stone corridor with a total length of 15.8 meters. The chamber uses massive stones for construction and faces southwest. Artifacts such as fragments of stone coffins, gold rings, iron items, Sue ware, and Haji ware were discovered during excavations.
The construction of this kofun is estimated to date back to the late 6th century, during the latter part of the Kofun period. It is believed to have served as the burial site of a prominent local clan, possibly associated with the immigrant Hata clan, known for their role in developing the Uzumasa and Saga regions.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a lateral stone corridor, and its specifications are as follows: * Total length: 15.8 meters * Main chamber: 6.4 meters long, 4.0 meters wide, and 5.2 meters high * Corridor: 9.2 meters long, 2.3 meters wide, and 2.3 meters high

'''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The **Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun**, located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the **late Kofun period**, it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a **two-sided horizontal stone chamber**, opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware}} and {{ill|Haji ware}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the **late 6th century**, preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the **Hata clan**, a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * **1912 (Meiji 12):** Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * **1941 (Showa 16):** The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * **1978 (Showa 53):** Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * **1980 (Showa 55):** Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * **1986 (Showa 61):** General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The **two-sided horizontal stone chamber** is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * **Main chamber (玄室):** Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * **Entrance passage (羨道):** Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a **stone coffin**, gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan burial mounds]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]]

Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a **stone coffin**, gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan burial mounds]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]]

'''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The **Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun**, located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The **Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun**, located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the **late Kofun period**, it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a **two-sided horizontal stone chamber**, opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the **late 6th century**, preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the **Hata clan**, a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * **1912 (Meiji 12):** Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * **1941 (Showa 16):** The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * **1978 (Showa 53):** Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * **1980 (Showa 55):** Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * **1986 (Showa 61):** General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The **two-sided horizontal stone chamber** is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * **Main chamber (玄室):** Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * **Entrance passage (羨道):** Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a **stone coffin**, gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan burial mounds]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]]

(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan burial mounds]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]]

A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]]

A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]]

Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Narabigaoka 1 Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Narabigaoka 1 Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Narabigaoka 1 Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]]

Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Narabigaoka 1 Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Narabigaoka 1 Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Narabigaoka 1 Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[Category:new pages]]

'''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * (The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * (The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * (The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 burial mounds in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this burial mound stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue ware|en}} and {{ill|Haji ware|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major burial mounds in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Burial Mounds of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == *
