{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alias = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | group = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its scale. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == Burial Facility Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. Artifacts from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designated a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The former stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site designation1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following dimensions: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] (The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site other_designation1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed occupant = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site other_designation1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] (The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site other_designation1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site other_designation1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site other_designation1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site other_designation1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] (The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡|12=draft}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群|12=draft}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|12=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|12=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|12=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|12=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡|12=draft}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群|12=draft}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|12=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|12=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|12=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|12=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}}
