Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡|12=draft}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群|12=draft}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|12=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|12=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|12=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|12=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|12=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|12=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|12=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|12=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] {{citation needed|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|12=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|12=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|12=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|12=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] {{citation needed|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡|12=draft}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群|12=draft}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|12=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|12=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|12=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|12=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] {{citation needed|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft|12=draft}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡|12=draft|12=draft}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|12=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|12=draft|12=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft|12=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft|12=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|12=draft|12=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft|12=draft}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡|12=draft|12=draft}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|12=draft|12=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|12=draft|12=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft|12=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=draft|12=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|12=draft|12=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]]

高さ8m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 金環・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （推定）[[秦氏]]一族 |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 双ヶ岡
1号墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|0|50|N|135|41|50|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|0|59.81|N|135|41|59.30|E}} | label1=垂箕山 | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-東南東.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|0|25.57|N|135|42|43.45|E}} | label2=天塚 | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|0|30.51|N|135|42|44.42|E}} | label3=清水山 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|0|14.80|N|135|42|24.37|E}} | label4=段ノ山 | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|1|26.80|N|135|42|47.87|E}} | label5=双ヶ岡1号 | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|0|42.83|N|135|41|59.88|E}} | label6=蛇塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size6=20 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | label7=円山 | label-pos7=left | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=20 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|35|1|10.80|N|135|41|24.85|E}} | label8=甲塚 | label-pos8=left | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=20 }} '''双ヶ岡1号墳'''（ならびがおかいちごうふん、双ヶ丘1号墳）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]御室双岡町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。双ヶ岡古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+太秦・嵯峨野地域の主な古墳{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}} !期!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!石室全長!!築造時期!!陵墓・史跡 |- | ||[[垂箕山古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||65m|| ||5c末-6c初頭||[[宮内庁]]治定墓 |- |rowspan=2|3||rowspan=2|[[天塚古墳]]||rowspan=2|前方後円墳||rowspan=2|73m||(8.1m)||rowspan=2|6c前半||rowspan=2|国の[[史跡]] |- |(7.7m) |- |rowspan=4|4
・
5||[[清水山古墳]]||前方後円墳||57m|| ||rowspan=2|6c中葉-後半||（消滅） |- |段ノ山古墳||前方後円墳|| || ||（消滅） |- |'''双ヶ岡1号墳'''||[[円墳]]||44m||15.8m{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}||rowspan=2|6世紀後半||なし |- |衣笠山1号墳||円墳||26m||(6.0m)||なし |- |rowspan=6|6||[[蛇塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||75m||17.8m||rowspan=6|6c末-7c初頭||国の史跡 |- |[[円山古墳 (京都市)|円山古墳
（大覚寺1号墳）]]||円墳||50m||14.7m||[[陵墓参考地]] |- |[[狐塚古墳 (京都市)|狐塚古墳
（大覚寺4号墳）]]||円墳||28m||(12.8m)||なし |- |[[入道塚古墳|入道塚古墳
（大覚寺2号墳）]]||[[方墳]]||30m|||11.2m||陵墓参考地 |- |[[大覚寺古墳群|南天塚古墳
（大覚寺3号墳）]]||円墳||25-30m||8.1m||（埋没） |- |御堂ヶ池1号墳||円墳||30m||(8.3m)||京都市登録史跡 |- |rowspan=2|7||[[甲塚古墳 (京都市)|甲塚古墳]]||円墳||38m||14.4m||rowspan=2|7c前半||なし |- |広沢古墳||円墳||30m||12.0m||なし |}
[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、[[双ヶ丘]]の一ノ丘頂上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。双ヶ丘では、一ノ丘と二ノ丘の間の谷筋に9基、二ノ丘の頂上に1基、二ノ丘南裾から三ノ丘に13基の古墳計24基が分布するが、本古墳はそのうちで最大規模になる{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 墳形は円形で、直径44メートル・高さ8メートルを測る{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室全長15.8メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品の大部分は失われており、石室内の調査では石棺片のほか、金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]に先行する時期の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。なお、戦前に[[清原夏野]]（[[837年]]）の墓として顕彰されたことで、古墳前に石碑が建てられている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1912年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[清原夏野]]墓碑の建立{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）11月13日、国の[[名勝]]「雙ヶ岡」の指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1698|雙ヶ岡}}。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）、京都市による双ヶ丘の公有化（以後環境整備）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、双ヶ岡1号墳の環境整備に伴う発掘調査（京都市文化観光局、[[1981年]]に報告）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、双ヶ丘の一般開放{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|旧石室開口部（現在は閉塞）}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * 石室全長：15.8メートル * 玄室：長さ6.4メートル、幅4.0メートル、高さ5.2メートル（報告書では長さ6.1メートル、幅3.6メートル、高さ5.0メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ2.3メートル（報告書では長さ8.5メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ2.3メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） 玄室の奥壁は各1石の3段積み、側壁は4段積み、前壁は2段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1段、羨道の側壁は推定2段積みである{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。玄室右壁の一部が崩れて天井石の一部が傾くなど崩壊する危険があるため、調査後に玄室部分には土嚢が充填されている。 石室内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では[[凝灰岩]]製石棺片のほか金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のほか、[[平安時代]]の土器のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京都市設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=双ケ岡1号墳の発掘調査及び保存修景事業概要|title=名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告 昭和55年度|publisher=京都市文化観光局|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/news/leaflet/268.pdf 「双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓（リーフレット京都No.268 考古アラカルト51）」]}}京都市埋蔵文化財研究所・京都市考古資料館、2011年|ref={{Harvid|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}}}。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=双ヶ丘古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|双ヶ丘古墳群（平凡社）|1979}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=[[広瀬和雄]]|year=2013|chapter=山城・蛇塚古墳をめぐる二,三の問題|title=国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第178集|publisher=[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|広瀬和雄|2013}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[京都大学]]考古学研究会|author=|year=1971|chapter=各古墳の概要 > 双ケ岡1号墳|title=嵯峨野の古墳時代 -御堂ヶ池群集墳発掘調査報告-|publisher=京大考古学研究会出版事務局|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=京都市|author=|year=1983|chapter=古墳時代 > 右京区|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[双ヶ丘]] == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Narabigaoka Kofun Group|双ヶ岡古墳群}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=双ヶ岡古墳群 |1-1=太秦・嵯峨野古墳群 |1-2=京都府の古墳 |1-3=右京区の歴史 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならひかおかいちこうふん}} [[Category:太秦・嵯峨野古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:太秦]] 1号墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|0|50|N|135|41|50|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|0|59.81|N|135|41|59.30|E}} | label1=垂箕山 | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-東南東.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|0|25.57|N|135|42|43.45|E}} | label2=天塚 | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|0|30.51|N|135|42|44.42|E}} | label3=清水山 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|0|14.80|N|135|42|24.37|E}} | label4=段ノ山 | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|1|26.80|N|135|42|47.87|E}} | label5=双ヶ岡1号 | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|0|42.83|N|135|41|59.88|E}} | label6=蛇塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size6=20 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | label7=円山 | label-pos7=left | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=20 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|35|1|10.80|N|135|41|24.85|E}} | label8=甲塚 | label-pos8=left | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=20 }} '''双ヶ岡1号墳'''（ならびがおかいちごうふん、双ヶ丘1号墳）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]御室双岡町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。双ヶ岡古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+太秦・嵯峨野地域の主な古墳{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}} !期!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!石室全長!!築造時期!!陵墓・史跡 |- | ||[[垂箕山古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||65m|| ||5c末-6c初頭||[[宮内庁]]治定墓 |- |rowspan=2|3||rowspan=2|[[天塚古墳]]||rowspan=2|前方後円墳||rowspan=2|73m||(8.1m)||rowspan=2|6c前半||rowspan=2|国の[[史跡]] |- |(7.7m) |- |rowspan=4|4
・
5||[[清水山古墳]]||前方後円墳||57m|| ||rowspan=2|6c中葉-後半||（消滅） |- |段ノ山古墳||前方後円墳|| || ||（消滅） |- |'''双ヶ岡1号墳'''||[[円墳]]||44m||15.8m{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}||rowspan=2|6世紀後半||なし |- |衣笠山1号墳||円墳||26m||(6.0m)||なし |- |rowspan=6|6||[[蛇塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||75m||17.8m||rowspan=6|6c末-7c初頭||国の史跡 |- |[[円山古墳 (京都市)|円山古墳
（大覚寺1号墳）]]||円墳||50m||14.7m||[[陵墓参考地]] |- |[[狐塚古墳 (京都市)|狐塚古墳
（大覚寺4号墳）]]||円墳||28m||(12.8m)||なし |- |[[入道塚古墳|入道塚古墳
（大覚寺2号墳）]]||[[方墳]]||30m|||11.2m||陵墓参考地 |- |[[大覚寺古墳群|南天塚古墳
（大覚寺3号墳）]]||円墳||25-30m||8.1m||（埋没） |- |御堂ヶ池1号墳||円墳||30m||(8.3m)||京都市登録史跡 |- |rowspan=2|7||[[甲塚古墳 (京都市)|甲塚古墳]]||円墳||38m||14.4m||rowspan=2|7c前半||なし |- |広沢古墳||円墳||30m||12.0m||なし |}
[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、[[双ヶ丘]]の一ノ丘頂上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。双ヶ丘では、一ノ丘と二ノ丘の間の谷筋に9基、二ノ丘の頂上に1基、二ノ丘南裾から三ノ丘に13基の古墳計24基が分布するが、本古墳はそのうちで最大規模になる{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 墳形は円形で、直径44メートル・高さ8メートルを測る{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室全長15.8メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品の大部分は失われており、石室内の調査では石棺片のほか、金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]に先行する時期の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。なお、戦前に[[清原夏野]]（[[837年]]）の墓として顕彰されたことで、古墳前に石碑が建てられている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1912年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[清原夏野]]墓碑の建立{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）11月13日、国の[[名勝]]「雙ヶ岡」の指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1698|雙ヶ岡}}。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）、京都市による双ヶ丘の公有化（以後環境整備）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、双ヶ岡1号墳の環境整備に伴う発掘調査（京都市文化観光局、[[1981年]]に報告）{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、双ヶ丘の一般開放{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|旧石室開口部（現在は閉塞）}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口した（調査後に埋め戻し）。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 * 石室全長：15.8メートル * 玄室：長さ6.4メートル、幅4.0メートル、高さ5.2メートル（報告書では長さ6.1メートル、幅3.6メートル、高さ5.0メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ2.3メートル（報告書では長さ8.5メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ2.3メートル{{Sfn|名勝双ケ岡保存整備事業報告|1981}}） 玄室の奥壁は各1石の3段積み、側壁は4段積み、前壁は2段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1段、羨道の側壁は推定2段積みである{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。玄室右壁の一部が崩れて天井石の一部が傾くなど崩壊する危険があるため、調査後に玄室部分には土嚢が充填されている。 石室内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では[[凝灰岩]]製石棺片のほか金環・鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片のほか、[[平安時代]]の土器のみが検出されている{{Sfn|双ヶ岡一の丘の首長墓|2011}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京都市設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡|qq=draft}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群|qq=draft}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|qq=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|qq=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|qq=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|qq=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|qq=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳|version=99996034|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]]

Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ukyo Ward|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft}}, Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}} | era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft}} | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft}} | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡|qq=draft}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群|qq=draft}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|qq=draft}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|qq=draft}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|qq=draft}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|qq=draft}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|qq=draft}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|qq=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳|version=99996034|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]]

Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzumasa|qq=}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano|qq=}} area, such as the {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|1=Hebizuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jazuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hebizuka Kofun|qq=}}. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|qq=}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|qq=}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic spot|qq=}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narabigaoka|qq=}} * {{ill|Jazuka Kofun|ja|蛇塚古墳|1=Hebizuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jazuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hebizuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳|version=99996034|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]]

The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzumasa|qq=}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano|qq=}} area, such as the [[:en:Hebizuka Kofun|Hebizuka Kofun]]. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|qq=}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|qq=}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic spot|qq=}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narabigaoka|qq=}} * [[:en:Hebizuka Kofun|Hebizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳|version=99996034|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:Round Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''' (双ヶ岡1号墳, 双ヶ丘1号墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | image = [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Kofun
(The stone monument for {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|qq=}} in the foreground was erected during the Meiji period.) | alternate_name = Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]], Omuro Narabioka-cho | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|27.82|N|135|42|46.95|E|region:JP-26}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = Diameter 44m, Height 8m | burial = Two-sided {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Gold rings, Iron items, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | era = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | presumed Interred_individual = (Hypothesis) Clan [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] | cultural property = None }} == Overview == [[File:Narabigaoka 1go-fun, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The typeer stone chamber entrance (now sealed).}}]] The '''Narabigaoka No. 1 Kofun''', located on the summit of the first hill of {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ岡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narabigaoka|qq=}}, is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|ja|双ヶ丘古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narabigaoka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Of the 24 Kofuns in the cluster—nine between the first and second hills, one at the summit of the second hill, and 13 from the southern base of the second hill to the third hill—this Kofun stands out for its map_size. Constructed during the '''late Kofun period''', it measures 44 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height. The tomb's burial facility is a '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''', opening towards the southwest. The chamber spans a total length of 15.8 meters and incorporates large stones in its construction. burial goods from the tomb include gold rings, fragments of iron tools, and sherds of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, though much of the contents were lost due to prior looting. The construction of this tomb is dated to the '''late 6th century''', preceding other major Kofuns in the {{ill|Uzumasa|ja|太秦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzumasa|qq=}}-{{ill|Sagano|ja|嵯峨野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano|qq=}} area, such as the [[:en:Hebizuka Kofun|Hebizuka Kofun]]. As such, it is speculated to be the grave of a leader of the '''Hata clan''', a group of foreign origin responsible for developing the Sagano region. A stone monument commemorating {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|qq=}}, who died in 837, was erected at the site during the pre-war period. == Archaeological History == * '''1912 (Meiji 12):''' Monument to {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|qq=}} erected. * '''1941 (Showa 16):''' The site Historic Site1 a national {{ill|Scenic spot|ja|名勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic spot|qq=}} ("Narabigaoka Scenic Area") on November 13. * '''1978 (Showa 53):''' Public acquisition of Narabigaoka by Kyoto City, followed by environmental restoration efforts. * '''1980 (Showa 55):''' Excavations and conservation work conducted alongside site improvements; results reported in 1981. * '''1986 (Showa 61):''' General public access to Narabigaoka opened. == burial Details == The '''two-sided horizontal stone chamber''' is constructed of tuff stone blocks, with the following area: * Total chamber length: 15.8 meters * '''Main chamber (玄室):''' Length 6.4 meters, Width 4.0 meters, Height 5.2 meters * '''Entrance passage (羨道):''' Length 9.2 meters, Width 2.3 meters, Height 2.3 meters The chamber's structure exhibits three tiers of stacked stones for the rear wall and four tiers for the side walls. Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narabigaoka|qq=}} * [[:en:Hebizuka Kofun|Hebizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳|version=99996034|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]]

Due to damage and erosion, certain areas were stabilized with sandbags following the 1980 investigation. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of a '''stone coffin''', gold rings, and various pottery pieces, although many items were looted prior to the modern investigation. == References == * Kyoto City Historical Research Board: Site Report from 1981 * "Kofuns of Narabigaoka: Leaflet No. 268," Kyoto Archaeological Institute, 2011. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Narabigaoka|ja|双ヶ丘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narabigaoka|qq=}} * [[:en:Hebizuka Kofun|Hebizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳|version=99996034|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun period sites in Japan]] [[Category:Hata clan Kofuns]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Narabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[ja:双ヶ岡1号墳]] [[sn:arabigaoka 1 Kofun]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:太秦]] [[Category:太秦・嵯峨野古墳群]]
